segments.text: After two days was the feast of the passover, and of unleavened bread: and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might take him by craft, and put him to death.
Not on the feast day, lest there be an uproar of the people.
segments.speaker: Chief Priests and Scribes

segments.text: And being in Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at meat, there came a woman having an alabaster box of ointment of spikenard very precious; and she brake the box, and poured it on his head.
Why was this waste of the ointment made?
segments.speaker: Some Disciples

segments.text: For it might have been sold for more than three hundred pence, and have been given to the poor. And they murmured against her.
Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went unto the chief priests, to betray him unto them.
Where wilt thou that we go and prepare that thou mayest eat the passover?
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: And as they sat and did eat, Jesus said,
Verily I say unto you, One of you which eateth with me shall betray me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And as they did eat, Jesus took bread, and blessed, and brake it, and gave to them, and said,
Take, eat: this is my body.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And when they had sung an hymn, they went out into the mount of Olives.
Although all shall be offended, yet will not I.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And immediately, while he yet spake, cometh Judas, one of the twelve, and with him a great multitude with swords and staves, from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.
And they led Jesus away to the high priest: and with him were assembled all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes.
And as Peter was beneath in the palace, there cometh one of the maids of the high priest:
